grid.row_distribution_comment: Each spin, each column independently re-rolls its row height from this distribution. Heights chosen to deliver Mahjong-Ways-feel variance.

paytable.comment: Ways paytable. Payout = (ways_count) * (per_k payout) * (current cascade multiplier). Ways count = product of symbol counts per reel for the k reels hit from leftmost.

multiplier_ladder.comment: Base game ladder. Same shape as MVP #2 cluster cascade for design consistency.

player_state_map.tension: variable reel heights tease before each drop (which reel is going to be tall?)
player_state_map.building_hope: multiplier climbs 2x -> 3x -> 5x AND row heights re-roll between cascades
player_state_map.peak_anticipation: 5+ cascade chain at 10x sustained with tall reels (high ways count)
player_state_map.climax: high ways_count on H1 at 10x multiplier inside FS climbing multiplier (top hit potential ~8000-10000x bet)
player_state_map.re_engagement: FS climbing multiplier never resets within FS session; long FS sessions are the dopamine reservoir

pseudocode: Each spin: re-roll row heights per column from row_distribution. Draw one symbol per (col, row) cell from that column's reel strip. Ways evaluation, leftmost-anchored: for each paying symbol, count occurrences per reel (WILDs substitute); ways_count = product over the k consecutive winning reels (from reel 0) where count>=1; pay paytable[symbol][k] * ways_count, then multiply by current cascade multiplier. After all symbol wins paid: remove winning cells, drop columns, refill from reel strip, regenerate row heights for refilled columns, increment cascade multiplier, re-evaluate. Repeat until no win. 4+ SCAT in initial drop trigger 12 free spins. In FS, multiplier persists and climbs +1 per winning cascade, never resets. Retrigger: 3+ SCAT adds 5 free spins.
engine_support: v0.2 (variable-height ways + cascade + scatter + FS climbing multiplier). v0.1 engine cannot run this; JSON spec is decision-ready.
notes: MVP #3 = dynamic-grid ways with cascade and persistent FS multiplier. This is the strongest direct answer to Chris's pipeline-mechanism request: it literally is a dynamic grid (variable column heights every spin) with cascade-based anticipation.
